title: Love and War in Classical Indian Literature (5) SSc/A&H
catalogDescription: Introduction to enduring themes of love and war in classical Indian literature in its cultural context with a specific focus on contemporary relevance, including popular culture. Texts in English translation.

title: Modern Literature of South Asia (5) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: Introduction to the modern literature of South Asia (India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal), from the eighteenth century to the present. Readings include a selection of novels, short stories, and poetry drawn from the diverse literary traditions of the region and placed in their cultural contexts. All texts in English translation.

title: Indian Philosophical Literature (5) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: Introduction to various topics pertaining to the vast philosophical literature of India, such as its origins and contexts, dharma; karma and free will; logic and argument. A variety of systems from Hindu, Buddhist, Jain, theistic, and non-theistic schools are covered. Taught in English. Offered: WS.

title: Fairies, Genies, and Monsters: The Romance Genre in India
catalogDescription: Introduction to the long adventure narratives (dastan) of South Asia, with reference to the Middle and Near East. Texts in English translation.
